nodes.title: 1/3rds full jug
nodes.summary: The 1/3rds full jug is a quest item in The Fremennik Trials quest. It is made by using a full jug on an empty bucket, then filling up the jug again and pouring it into the bucket. Using it on a 3/5ths full bucket makes the 4/5ths full bucket needed to advance the quest.

nodes.title: 1/5ths full bucket
nodes.summary: A 1/5ths full bucket is used in Peer the Seer's trial for the quest The Fremennik Trials.

nodes.title: 10th squad sigil
nodes.summary: The 10th squad sigil is an item used to teleport to the final boss during the Monkey Madness I quest. It will no longer teleport you to the boss fight arena after you defeat the Jungle Demon. It can be safely thrown away after the quest, however it can be kept as a cosmetic item. If lost, the sigil may be recovered by speaking to Waymottin, the gnome mage at the end of the Ape Atoll Dungeon. However, if you have completed Monkey Madness II, it can be reclaimed by Zooknock upstairs in the house south-west of Anita's. If lost due to death, you may reclaim it by speaking to Death. Attempting to equip the sigil in the Wilderness during the quest will result in a message saying You cannot teleport out of the Wilderness using the sigil.

nodes.title: 2/3rds full jug
nodes.summary: The 2/3rds full jug is a quest item found in The Fremennik Trials quest. This is obtained by using a full bucket on an empty jug, emptying the jug in the drain, then using the 2/5ths full bucket again onto the empty jug.

nodes.title: 2/5ths full bucket
nodes.summary: A 2/5ths full bucket is used in Peer the Seer's trial for the quest The Fremennik Trials.

nodes.title: 200 million experience
nodes.summary: 200 million experience is the maximum amount of experience that a player can obtain in any one skill. This amount of experience represents a large time investment, with each skill taking hundreds or thousands of hours of efficient playing. At current rates, obtaining 200 million experience in all skills takes approximately 13,000 hours of playing at minimum, using the best methods available. The first player to achieve 200 million experience in all skills was Lynx Titan on 28 December 2018, approximately six years after Old School RuneScape's release. The first ironman to achieve the same feat was Iron Hyger on 1 December 2022, nearly four years after Lynx Titan, and just over 8 years after the release of the ironman game mode. In-game, having 200 million experience in a skill provides no practical reward or benefit, apart from making the player fifteen times more likely to receive a skilling pet in the appropriate skill. There are no notifications for achieving 200 million experience, although the first two to achieve it in all skills received a chatbox message saying "Congratulations, [player name]!" accompanied by a picture of beer. 200 million experience was chosen as the maximum amount due to experience being stored as a 32-bit signed integer with one decimal point: the maximum experience that could be stored is 214,748,364.7, although it was limited to a round number.

nodes.title: 3rd age robe top (Last Man Standing)
nodes.summary: 3rd age robe top from Last Man Standing is a minigame-exclusive variant of the normal 3rd age robe top. It can be obtained from opening the chests with a bloody key, or looting the crates that spawn at random locations on the map. It is dropped on death to whoever killed you.

nodes.title: 3rd age vambraces
nodes.summary: The 3rd age vambraces are a part of the 3rd age range set. Requiring level 65 Ranged and 45 Defence to wear, they offer similar bonuses to blessed vambraces with an additional +1 to magic defence instead of a +1 prayer bonus like blessed. The 3rd age range vambraces are a possible reward from hard, elite and master tier Treasure Trails and can't be made through any skills. This item can be stored in the treasure chest of a costume room, provided that the interface of the corresponding clue tier is opened before attempting to store the item. Alternatively, the item can be used on the opened treasure chest to insert it without viewing the interface.

nodes.title: 3rd age wand
nodes.summary: The 3rd age wand is a part of the 3rd age mage set and tied second-strongest wand in the game. It is a possible reward from elite and master Treasure Trails and requires 65 Magic to wield. The 3rd age wand provides the same combat bonuses as a master wand, but is significantly heavier, requires an additional 5 Magic levels to wield, and unlike the master wand, cannot autocast Ancient Magicks or the Arceuus spellbook. This item can be stored in the treasure chest of a costume room, provided that the interface of the corresponding clue tier is opened before attempting to store the item. Alternatively, the item can be used on the opened treasure chest to insert it without viewing the interface.

nodes.title: 4/5ths full bucket
nodes.summary: A 4/5ths full bucket is used in Peer the Seer's trial for the quest The Fremennik Trials. This is used on the chest with the scale on top to receive a vase.

nodes.title: 5-gallon jug
nodes.summary: The 5-gallon jug is a quest item in The Forsaken Tower quest. It is found in a cupboard on the ground floor and is used to solve the puzzle to obtain Dinh's hammer.

nodes.title: 8-gallon jug
nodes.summary: The 8-gallon jug is an item obtained during The Forsaken Tower. It is found in a cupboard on the ground floor, and is used to solve a puzzle to obtain Dinh's hammer.

nodes.title: ??? mixture
nodes.summary: The ??? mixture is a quest item used only in Recruitment Drive. It is made by incorrectly mixing ingredients together during Miss Cheevers's trial. There are three ??? mixtures obtainable during the quest, none of which are useful in completing the challenge. Because many of the ingredients cannot be retrieved after use, it is likely that the challenge must be restarted if a ??? mixture is created. The hot mixture is made by using cupric sulfate on the vial of liquid. The warm mixture is made by using gypsum on a vial of liquid. The horrible mixture is made by adding cupric sulfate to anything else, or to another hot mixture.

nodes.title: A Doubt
nodes.summary: A Doubt is a monster fought during the Dream Mentor quest in the Dream World. Many Doubts are summoned during the first battle by The Inadequacy. They represent Cyrisus, the protagonist, and his little nagging doubts about combat. Though they can be fought and killed easily, they disappear once the player defeats The Inadequacy. It is recommended to turn off auto-retaliate to avoid attacking Doubts, and avoid attacking them at all unless using blood spells to heal.

nodes.title: A container
nodes.summary: A container is an item that players create as part of Animal Magnetism. They make it by combining a pattern with polished buttons and hard leather. They then give it to Ava, who turns it into Ava's attractor. The container is important in finishing the quest and is the structure of the whole attractor.

nodes.title: A corpse
nodes.summary: A corpse is a monster that spawns after drinking the bottle of graveyard corpse reviver. Damage from the corpse can be prevented by safespotting it behind a facility and logging out and back in, which will despawn the monster. Every time it is defeated, it has a chance to revive with full HP with the message "The corpse seems to regenerate again!". It can revive up to 9 times before being truly defeated.

nodes.title: A dark disposition
nodes.summary: A dark disposition is a quest reward for completing The Ascent of Arceuus. It is used on Kharedst's memoirs to allow teleportation to Arceuus. If lost before being added to Kharedst's memoirs, a replacement can be claimed from Lord Trobin Arceuus in the upper level of the Tower of Magic. After completing the quest A Kingdom Divided, a replacement can be claimed from Lord Trobin's son, Asteros Arceuus, on the 1st floor of Kourend Castle. Note that Asteros will only give it to the player after talking to him twice. Using Kharedst's memoirs after adding this page to it on Alyssa results in unique dialogue, revealing that she is the handmaid named Alyssa in the story.

nodes.title: A dear friend
nodes.summary: A dear friend is a book found in the Grand Library in Prifddinas, which requires completion of Song of the Elves to access. The book is written before the First Age prior to the elves' arrival on Gielinor from their homeworld Tarddiad. It describes the experiences of Caron and his wife's encounter with a Hunllef cub, the elves' natural predator, on Tarddiad. If lost, it can be reclaimed from any bookshelf in the Grand Library, or from a bookcase within a player-owned house.

nodes.title: A handwritten book
nodes.summary: A handwritten book is a book written by Oaknock the Engineer that is found in the drawers near Brimstail in his cave. It is a word-for-word transcription of the book Crystal singing for beginners that players receive from Ilfeen.

nodes.title: Abyssal walker (The Scar)
nodes.summary: Abyssal Walkers are found during the Desert Treasure II quest when entering The Scar for the first time. When entering the scar, players are met with various monsters fighting each other, including some abyssal walkers. Kill all monsters to proceed with the quest.

nodes.title: Achilka
nodes.summary: Achilka is a boater who offers free transport services on the River Varla and its tributaries. Her destinations are Kastori, Tal Teklan, and the Gloomthorn Trail in Auburn Valley.

nodes.title: Acidic Araxyte
nodes.summary: Acidic Araxytes are a type of araxyte who hatch from green eggs during the battle with Araxxor. They use accurate ranged attacks that can deal up to 15 damage, and scatters acid pools around it upon death. Players will always max hit when attacking them with a crush or heavy Ranged weapon (such as a crossbow or ballista) if crush or ranged is their highest accuracy style respectively, or the noxious halberd. Araxxor's minions and eggs are considered by the game to be part of an araxyte task without decrementing the task count, allowing the accuracy and damage buff of the Slayer helm to be utilised against them.

nodes.title: Acidic Bloodveld
nodes.summary: The Acidic Bloodveld is a monster encountered while fighting Vanstrom Klause during Sins of the Father. During the fight, Vanstrom will occasionally say: My pets will feast on your corpse! which summons the bloodveld. The bloodveld should be killed from a distance using a ranged or magic attack, or else it will explode for 35-50 damage. When the bloodveld is killed, a pool of acid appears on the ground. Stepping on the pool of acid will deal additional damage.

nodes.title: Adala
nodes.summary: Adala is working as the hired carrier for Patzi when starting the Death on the Isle quest. After Livius' death during the quest, Adala is one of the suspects to the murder. She is pickpocketed to obtain wine labels, leading to the reveal that she is a thief trying to steal a container of Principum Red from the wine cellar. She is also briefly fought after the player accuses her of murder, though the combat will end before either she or the player dies.

nodes.title: Adamant dragon
nodes.summary: Adamant dragons are metal dragons first encountered during Dragon Slayer II, in which the player must fight against one during Zorgoth's assault. After completion of the quest, they can be found in the western chamber of the Lithkren Vault. To get back to the laboratory underneath Lithkren, players can travel using the rowboat from the quest or, after completion, taking a digsite pendant and using it on the strange machine north of the rune dragon room, adding a teleport straight to Lithkren on it. Adamant dragons are the result of a side project of Zorgoth's after being inspired by another dragonkin, Forcae, which involves infusing dragon eggs with various metals. While Forcae's metal dragons were created to find a solution to the dragonkin's infertility, Zorgoth had bred his metal dragons for his war against humanity.

nodes.title: Adventurers' Camp (Lumbridge Swamp)
nodes.summary: The Adventurers' Camp is a small camp located in Lumbridge Swamp at the threshold between the forest and the swamp between Draynor Village and Lumbridge and north of Father Urhney's house. It has a monk, wizard, archer, and warrior. The monk, wizard, and archer will not talk to the player much, but talking to the warrior will clumsily reveal their intentions to find Zanaris and start the Lost City quest.

nodes.title: Aehecatl
nodes.summary: Aehecatl is part of the Sunset Ocean, lying west of Varlamore. To the south is Dusk's Maw and to the north is Aestuarium Tempestus. The city of Tal Teklan lies along Aehecatl's shore. A salvaging spot with merchant shipwrecks can be found here, to the south. From the shore, north of Tal Teklan, a fishing spot can be accessed. Aehecatl is full of jagged reefs, and as such is non-traversible; while the player can slowly make headway through this hazard if they bring a boat with high hitpoints alongside repair kits, players will eventually reach an invisible barrier within the expanse that stops boats from moving further.

nodes.title: Aemad's Adventuring Supplies.
nodes.summary: Aemad's Adventuring Supplies is a general store in East Ardougne. The store is run by its owner, Aemad, and his assistant, Kortan. Although it does not sell the default items of a typical general store, it sells several items used in quests around RuneScape, notably rope. Two nearby banks (Ardougne north bank and Ardougne south bank) make this general store useful for buying and banking vials of water. The Ardougne Market is also nearby, making this store handy for selling items stolen from stalls. This general store is particularly useful to Ultimate Ironmen; both an axe and a pickaxe may be quickly obtained after an Ardougne Teleport teleports the caster to the nearby Ardougne market. Additionally, the quick access to balls of wool makes this store helpful to quickly string amulets.

nodes.title: Aestuarium Tempestus
nodes.summary: Aestuarium Tempestus is part of the Sunset Ocean, located west of Tempestus and south of the Stranglewood. It cannot currently be accessed.

nodes.title: Afflicted
nodes.summary: The afflicted are villagers of Mort'ton who were cursed by a strange mist sent from the Sanguinesti region by Lord Lowerniel Vergidiyad Drakan. They speak nonsense to the player if talked to. Afflicted can be cured temporarily by using either Serum 207 or Serum 208. Even though Serum 208 is supposed to be a "permanent" cure, ordinary villagers will always return to their afflicted state.

nodes.title: Afon Ganol
nodes.summary: Afon Ganol is a river that runs horizontally through the middle of Tirannwn, going from Arandar into the Western Ocean.

nodes.title: Agelus' Farm Shop
nodes.summary: Agelus' Farm Shop is a Farming shop located at the Ortus Farm west of Civitas illa Fortis. It is run by Agelus and it sells Farming supplies.

nodes.title: Agile Scarab
nodes.summary: Agile Scarabs are a monster hatched from eggs laid by Kephri in the Tombs of Amascut. They retreat from players at running speed, unlike most other monsters in the game.

nodes.title: Agile Warrior
nodes.summary: The Agile Warrior is a support summoned by Surok Magis during his confrontation in While Guthix Sleeps, noted by him shouting Fighting you is beneath me!, which summons either him or the Strong Warrior; when summoned, the agile warrior will shout My nimbleness is yours, master! The agile warrior is able to bind players upon reaching them, allowing Surok to cast an earthquake spell that deals up to 55 damage. Attacking him head on will cause him to dodge the player's attacks, requiring Bind, Snare, or Entangle to be cast on them before they can be properly killed off.

nodes.title: Agility
nodes.summary: Agility is a members-only skill which gives access to various shortcuts around Gielinor. Other benefits of the skill include faster run energy restoration, slower run energy dissipation while running, and the ability to wear weight-saving outfits like the graceful outfit, as well as wield certain tools and weaponry. Note that the passive benefits of faster energy restore and lower run energy drain rate also apply in free-to-play worlds.

nodes.title: Agility Arena Store
nodes.summary: The Agility Arena Store is the rewards shop for the Brimhaven Agility Arena minigame, run by Pirate Jackie the Fruit at the minigame's entrance. Players can exchange Agility arena tickets for 345 Agility experience each (379.5 experience each with Karamja gloves 2 or higher equipped), and Brimhaven vouchers for items and a recolour of the graceful outfit. When buying the toadflax or snapdragon from the store, it will come noted.

nodes.title: Agility Pyramid
nodes.summary: The Agility Pyramid, also known as the Jaleustrophos Pyramid (meaning Tomb of the Agile in an old desert language), is an Agility training area located in the Kharidian Desert, between Sophanem and Nardah, available to players with level 30 Agility. On this course, players start at the bottom of a pyramid with the goal of reaching the top, where they are able to collect the pyramid top. Players have to successfully traverse each of the five layers of the pyramid, which are filled with obstacles that force the player back to the previous layer upon failure to pass them. Pyramid tops may be handed in to Simon Templeton in exchange for 10,000 coins each and respawn only when the player has entered the doorway at the top of the pyramid. Level 75 Agility or higher ensures success on all of the obstacles, making it possible to complete a maximum of 26 laps per hour, which rewards a maximum of 44,700 experience per hour.

nodes.title: Agility Pyramid mine
nodes.summary: The Agility Pyramid mine is located just north of the Wanderer's tent from Icthlarin's Little Helper. It contains five gold rocks. Due to the distance from any bank and the damaging desert heat, it is not considered a very good place to gather gold ore. The closest bank is at Sophanem, which is only accessible after the completion of the Contact! quest. Players can bank easily in Sophanem after completing the quests Contact! and Icthlarin's Little Helper through entering the "rock" just to the south, which brings them directly into Sophanem into the building just beside where they emerge.

nodes.title: Agmundi Quality Clothes
nodes.summary: Agmundi Quality Clothes is a store specialising in clothes designed for dwarves, although the owner Agmundi will sell to the few Humans who pass through Keldagrim. The shop stocks a supply of clothes made by Agmundi's sister, Vermundi. She stocks higher quality clothes than Vermundi, but at a higher price. The clothes Agmundi stocks come in three colours; blue, pink and tan-yellow. Her shop is located in the ground floor of the marketplace in the north-west corner. Agmundi will also give out tasks to players.

nodes.title: Alice's farm
nodes.summary: Alice's farm is a farm located west of Port Phasmatys and east of Canifis. It's managed by Alice and her now-ethereal husband Malcolm. The farm is involved in the Animal Magnetism quest and also the locations of several undead chickens and undead cows. Alice's Farming Shop can be accessed by either talking or right-clicking Alice. West of the farm, one can find two allotments, a herb patch, and a flower patch. Alice's daughter, Lyra, attends the allotments for a payment. There's also a tool leprechaun and a compost bin.

nodes.title: Allanna's Farming Shop
nodes.summary: Allanna's Farming Shop is a farming shop run by Allanna, located in the Farming Guild. It sells an assortment of farming tools and supplies.

nodes.title: Alloviz Estuary
nodes.summary: Alloviz Estuary is a location south of Port Phasmatys. It opens into the Gravewater Bay. The estuary is the result of drainage from the Mort Myre Swamp and Haunted Woods.

nodes.title: Alomone
nodes.summary: Alomone is the leader of the Cult of Hazeel, a group dedicated to the revival of the Zamorakian mahjarrat Hazeel. The cult stole a set of armour from Ceril Carnillean then broke in four more times in search of the scroll required to revive Hazeel, but failed each time. In Hazeel Cult, Ceril commissions the player to retrieve the armour, pointing them to the cult's lair in the sewers. Players may choose to side with Hazeel during the Hazeel Cult quest, in which Alomone will accept Clivet's recommendation to let the player join the cult, welcoming them as the newest member of the Cult of Hazeel. He tasks them with finding the scroll needed to revive Hazeel, which they manage to do, allowing the cult to revive Hazeel. If the player chooses not to side with him, Alomone must be killed later on in the quest in order to retrieve the stolen armour from a nearby chest. He can be recoiled. The drop trick can be used to obtain more than one by looting the chest multiple times. He also plays a small role in Secrets of the North if the player sided with Hazeel, summoning both Hazeel when the player requests him to, and General Khazard when requested to by Hazeel.

nodes.title: Alrik
nodes.summary: Alrik is a subject of Miscellania located in the Miscellania market. Killing him (or any other subject of Miscellania) will result in losing 4.72% Managing Miscellania favour.

nodes.title: Alry the Angler's Angling Accessories
nodes.summary: Alry the Angler's Angling Accessories is a shop run by Alry the Angler that uses Molch pearls obtained from aerial fishing. Players can buy equipable versions of fishing rods, as well as a cosmetic fish sack that can be combined with the fish barrel to make the fish sack barrel. The shop also offers Angler's outfit pieces as an alternative to obtaining them from the Fishing Trawler minigame. The pearl barbarian rod is only available after partial completion of the Barbarian Training miniquest.

nodes.title: Amelia's Seed Shop
nodes.summary: Amelia's Seed Shop is a seed shop run by Amelia, located in the Farming Guild. It sells an assortment of allotment, flower, and hops seeds. Her stock is the same as the one in Draynor Market, but with slightly lower prices.

nodes.title: Amlodd's Magical Supplies
nodes.summary: Amlodd's Magical Supplies is a rune shop run by Efa on the north-west side of Prifddinas. It is one of few shops to stock cosmic runes, and the only shop to stock more than 30.

nodes.title: Ammonite Crab
nodes.summary: Ammonite Crabs can be found on most of the coastal areas and northern beaches of Fossil Island, as well as in the northern Wyvern Cave.

nodes.title: Amoxliatl
nodes.summary: Amoxliatl is a powerful Frost Nagua found within the Ruins of Tapoyauik, who serves as its guardian and is fought during The Heart of Darkness as the final fight of the quest. After the quest, Amoxliatl can be fought again as a regular boss. Like Scurrius, she is considered an entry-level boss with attacks meant to introduce various mechanics that are present in higher-level bosses, such as quickly reacting to prayers being deactivated and controlling where persistent ground hazards appear, which are comparable to Vorkath's corrupting dragonfire and the Phantom Muspah's ground spikes.

nodes.title: Amoxliatl (Echo)
nodes.summary: Echo Amoxliatl is a more challenging variant of Amoxliatl that is accessible during the Demonic Pacts League. To fight her the player must carry a Varlamore echo orb, which is dropped by the regular Amoxliatl boss. The unique drop of the echo version is the Infernal tecpatl.

nodes.title: Ancient Cavern
nodes.summary: The Ancient Cavern is an area reached from the whirlpool near Otto Godblessed's house. Players must dive into the whirlpool to enter the cavern, which contains many high-level monsters, including a high level metal dragon, the mithril dragon. The cavern can only be accessed after the player has completed the first part of Barbarian Firemaking, requiring level 35 Firemaking. After that, the player must ask Otto about making pyre ships, and then will be granted access to the dungeon in order to fulfil the objectives required to make them. If a player dives into the whirlpool before completing these tasks, they will be swept away, losing a small amount of their hitpoints, eventually washing up on the shore near Hadley's house. In order to leave the dungeon, the player must either teleport or use one of two aged logs near the body of water. The logs will bring the player to shore south-east of Baxtorian Falls. Dwarf multicannons cannot be used here. If attempted, players will receive the message: It's far too damp for you to set up a cannon here.

nodes.title: Ancient Custodian
nodes.summary: The Ancient Custodian is a superior variant of custodian stalkers. It has a chance of spawning after purchasing the unlock Bigger and Badder for 50 Slayer reward points from any Slayer master, as long as an Elder custodian stalker was killed. When an ancient Custodian spawns, a unique animation plays in which up to three other nearby Custodian stalkers will howl alongside it.

nodes.title: Ancient Guardian
nodes.summary: The Ancient Guardian is a quest monster that is encountered at the end of Below Ice Mountain. Players can choose to kill this golem through standard combat, but lower levelled players are recommended to mine the four structural pillars in the corners of the room; this will cause some rocks to fall down and damage it. Alternatively, the guardian can be safespotted by luring it to the eastern side of the chamber, then retreating into the entrance passage where it will get stuck on the corner. Despite being a golem, it is not weak to golembane weapons like the Barronite mace.

nodes.title: Ancient Guardian (Desert Treasure II)
nodes.summary: The Ancient Guardian is an enemy encountered during Desert Treasure II. It has a shield that must be depleted before it can take damage. Ancient Magicks may be used to deplete the shield but the guardian is immune to freezes from the spells themselves. Due to the speed at which the shield recharges, it is recommended to use a weapon with an attack speed of 4 ticks or less. Any missed attack with a weapon that takes 5 ticks or more will result in the shield starting to recharge before the next attack. The Ancient Guardian has a special attack where it will smash the ground. This will drop boulders from above, and can cause upwards of 25 damage if not avoided. This can be avoided by stepping away from the shadows that appear on the ground. One boulder will always be positioned on your current tile.

nodes.title: Ancient Guthixian Temple
nodes.summary: The Ancient Guthixian Temple is an ancient temple devoted to Guthix that was primarily built to house the Stone of Jas. The druids who built the temple created a complex system that was meant to keep intruders out. Found at the bottom of the Chasm of Tears, it is accessed by attracting a light creature with a sapphire lantern to ride down into the chasm. The player must then run south and climb up two stone walls to reach the entrance. Alternatively, players can teleport to the temple with a Guthixian temple teleport. During the events of While Guthix Sleeps, the whereabouts of the Stone of Jas are discovered to be at the bottom of the Chasm of Tears. The player, pretending to be Surok Magis in order to get past Movario and Darve, opens a huge door within the temple to reach the Stone of Jas. The Stone was protected by the Balance Elemental, a Guardian created by Guthix, and remained there since the end of the God Wars until it was stolen by Lucien, a Mahjarrat who also possesses the Staff of Armadyl. Once descending into the chasm players cannot set up a dwarf multicannon anywhere within the temple. Attempting to do so yields the chatbox message: "A strange magical force won't let you set up a cannon here."

nodes.title: Ancient Pyramid
nodes.summary: The Ancient Pyramid, also known as the Jaldraocht Pyramid, meaning Tomb of Magic in an old desert language, is located south of the Bandit Camp in the Kharidian Desert. It is a key location in the quest Desert Treasure I, and upon its completion, can be used by players to switch their spellbook to Ancient Magicks or back, as well as create magic tablets for Ancient Magicks teleports via the nearby lectern. During the quest, players must use the main entrance of the pyramid to get in, which requires them to run past attacking creatures (see below) while dodging traps. The pyramid has four levels, each of which is larger in width than the last. Some players consider getting through to be of some difficulty, as the traps will send the player back to the entrance. After the completion of the quest, players may use the south entrance to return to the centre of the pyramid without frustration. After completion of the Desert Treasure I quest, players can buy the ancient staff from Eblis for 80,000 coins. If they happen to lose it, they can buy one from another player or kill mummies inside the pyramid for another. Searching the sarcophagi within the pyramid (but outside the throne room) can yield small treasure such as sapphire rings, though it is definitely unwise to search the sarcophagi while running through the pyramid for Desert Treasure I.

nodes.title: Ancient Reef
nodes.summary: The Ancient Reef is part of the Untamed Ocean, found east of Lithkren. It is currently inaccessible.

nodes.title: Ancient Vault
nodes.summary: The Ancient Vault is an old Zarosian structure found in the eastern coast of the Kharidian Desert. The vault door, likely built during the reign of the Zarosian Empire in the Second Age, is the only entrance. The vault serves as the starting point for Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire. During the quest, it is revealed that the vault was holding the Elder Horn, which was brought there by Perseriya, admiral of the Zarosian fleet during the Third Age. The vault had impressive security capabilities; the first door would only allow those with Ancient Magicks in, implying that only Zarosians were allowed into the vault. The entrance to the lower level was sealed by a trapdoor, which would not open unless the four statues lining the room - statues of The Forgotten Four, four high-ranking Zarosian personnel, were fitted with their respective medallion. As an additional safety measure, the vault was surrounded by magic powerful enough to repel Mahjarrat, preventing them from teleporting in to access its contents, forcing them to open it normally. Once access to the lower vault was made, this restriction would be removed. After the quest, the vault can be visited again to speak with the Mysterious Bandit to buy additional rings of shadows. A chest can also be found here, which can be opened by using one of the ancient quartzes dropped by the Forgotten Four to receive one of five different common drops related to the boss.

nodes.title: Apparition
nodes.summary: Apparitions are the manifestations of some of the Menaphite Pantheon; specifically Het, Apmeken, Scabaras, and Crondis. They are fought during Icthlarin's Little Helper, depending on which canopic jar is taken during the quest. Note: If not killed within 10 minutes, the apparition will despawn.

nodes.title: Apple orchard
nodes.summary: An apple orchard can be found immediately south-east of the Tree Gnome Stronghold. Inside the orchard, players can find a Rotten Apple Pile. During Mourning's End Part I, players use a barrel on the pile to receive a barrel of rotten apples, and then use the resulting barrel on the nearby Apple Press to make an apple barrel. The scout from The General's Shadow miniquest can also be found wandering just outside of the orchard, to the west. Although the apple orchard was visible before Mourning's End Part I was released, it was inaccessible to players until then.

nodes.title: Aquanite
nodes.summary: Aquanites are Slayer monsters found in the Ynysdail Cavern beneath Ynysdail. They require level 78 Slayer to fight, and Ynysdail requires level 73 Sailing to access. Aquanites can be assigned by Nieve and Duradel after being unlocked for 80 Slayer reward points. There is also a task extension unlock available for 100 points. Aquanites can drop the unique aquanite tendon, used to make the aquanite hopper. They have a very high drop rate for coral frags compared to other monsters. If attacked with a weapon set to slash, their lure will be severed and their stab defence will be reduced by 50 (from 60 to 10), displaying the message Your attack cuts off the aquanite's lure! This attack does not need to deal damage to sever the lure. While on an aquanite slayer task, there is a 1/200 chance that an Elder Aquanite will spawn after you kill an aquanite (if you have Bigger and Badder unlocked).

nodes.title: Arandar
nodes.summary: Arandar is a mountain pass leading to Tirannwn that may be used by players upon completion of the Regicide quest. Players may use the pass to access the elven lands without having to travel through the Underground Pass again. Prior to completion, the huge gate is a one-way exit, leading to the forest south-west of the Tree Gnome Stronghold. Arandar is the only known overland crossing through the impassable mountain range that borders Kandarin. The Underground Pass is long and extremely dangerous, and was conquered by Iban long ago. There are dire wolves, deadly red spiders, and mourners wandering the Arandar path, although most adventurers using the pass will be strong enough to deal with them. The mourners on the path are involved in the Mourning's End Part I quest. While they are only level 11, they will throw a vial if attacked, which drastically lowers all combat stats and hitpoints down to 20 temporarily, however they are not a threat unless attacked. After completion of the Song of the Elves quest, no more mourners appear here. The mourner gear can be reobtained by searching the chest in front of the burgundy coloured bed under the Mourner Headquarters. There are a handful of Agility shortcuts within the pass that make it easier to traverse, requiring Agility levels of 59, 68, and 85.

nodes.title: Arandar mine
nodes.summary: The Arandar mine is located to the north of the exit from the pass in Tirannwn and to the east of Prifddinas' eastern wall. It is guarded by three dire wolves.

nodes.title: Araxxor
nodes.summary: Araxxor is the boss version of araxytes found within the Morytania Spider Cave, requiring level 92 Slayer and access to Morytania to kill. As a Slayer boss, he may only be killed while on a spider or araxyte Slayer task. Konar quo Maten can also assign Araxxor specifically when she assigns a boss task. Araxxor is currently the strongest Slayer boss in-game by combat level. He uniquely drops pieces of the noxious halberd, a strong two-handed slash weapon, and the araxyte fang, which can be combined with the amulet of torture to create the amulet of rancour, the best-in-slot melee amulet.

nodes.title: Araxyte
nodes.summary: Araxytes are a venomous species of arachnid found exclusively in the Morytania Spider Cave, requiring level 92 Slayer to kill. As they are abundant in numbers, able to inflict venom, and in a multicombat area, it is dangerous to fight them without prayer and a method to stave off venom. There are two forms of araxytes; a level 96 version which is 1x1, and a level 146 version which is 2x2 and has slightly higher stats. They are killed for their aranea boots drop, a hybrid set of boots versatile when using multiple combat styles in one setup. While both variants share the same drop table, the level 146 variants can be preferable to kill over the level 96 variants; their common drops are doubled, and the drop rate of the aranea boots are additionally doubled. They are also capable of spawning the Dreadborn Araxyte, something that the level 96 variants are incapable of doing. However, players may still choose to kill the level 96 variant, as their lower HP makes them one of the fastest slayer tasks. They play a minor role in the quest A Night at the Theatre, where players must steal some strange spider eggs from their cave.

nodes.title: Arboretum (location)
nodes.summary: The Arboretum is the "vault" for the infamous Blisterwood Tree, found in the upper tier of Darkmeyer. Lord Drakan had spent centuries attempting to destroy the tree, but failed. He simply locked it up in the building, forbidding anyone to enter it. It is meant to keep the Blisterwood Tree, an extremely dangerous wood to vampyres, out of accessible hands. During Sins of the Father, the Arboretum is guarded by Desmodus Lasiurus, who only lets the player in after helping him retrieve his slaves back from Lord Alexei Jovkai. During the quest, searching the shelves on the floor yields the old note.

nodes.title: Arcane Scarab
nodes.summary: Arcane Scarabs are a monster that is summoned by Kephri in the Tombs of Amascut. They slowly charge up red swarms around themselves. If allowed to fully charge, they will unleash the red swarm and deal massive damage, although it can be reduced by using Protect from Magic. After enough combat, the scarab will relocate, dispersing the red swarm around them. It is possible for the scarab to relocate if it takes 39 damage or more in a single hit. Otherwise, the scarab will relocate after 2 + N hits, where N is the amount of players in the raid. Missed attacks will contribute towards relocating the scarab whereas thrall attacks and burn damage do not.

nodes.title: Arceuus
nodes.summary: Arceuus (pronounced arr-KAY-us) is one of the five cities in the Kingdom of Great Kourend. Led by Lord Trobin Arceuus of the Arceuus House, the city is inhabited by Citizens of Arceuus, immortal beings who commit themselves to studying magic through the Dark Altar, a mysterious altar whose powers extend to life and death, expanding their Magic abilities and producing runes for the region. A majority of those who reside in Arceuus are former humans who, through the power of the Dark Altar, are able to achieve immortality by moving their soul from their mortal body into an incorporeal one. Their souls are freed from the mortal bodies in the crypt below the church, and take on the incorporeal form commonly seen among Arceuus residents. The art of runecrafting has been known by the Arceuus since at least 150 years before the wave of visitors from the mainland arrived. However, they were unable to create law runes, explaining the lack of teleportation spells, save for those used by Xeric. However, a book titled Transvergence Theory documents the differences between runecrafting in the mainland and runecrafting in Arceuus. While the mainland uses talismans to teleport individuals to another dimension, runecrafters in Arceuus use energy that emanates from the ground near the Dark Altar to change the essence. Arceuus is closest to the Northern Tundras of Great Kourend, and is the first line of defence against the Wintertodt, a mysterious entity that brings along with it extremely harsh cold.

nodes.title: Arceuus Library
nodes.summary: The Arceuus Library, also known as the Grand Library of Great Kourend, is a large library found in Arceuus. Created during the attack of the Wintertodt (known then as the Winter Beast) 1,360 years ago, its construction was ordered by Lord Arceuus I, who requested all citizens submit writing, texts, documents and research from all cities in order to preserve the history of Great Kourend. Important historical documents, such as the Official Order of Construction, are stored in the Library Historical Archive, a repurposed chamber of the Tower of Magic, for further study and additional protection. Players can access the Archive by speaking to Archeio on the ground floor. The library can quickly be reached by using the fairy code CIS.

nodes.title: Arceuus bank
nodes.summary: The Arceuus bank is a bank located in Arceuus.

nodes.title: Arceuus church
nodes.summary: The Arceuus church is a large church located in eastern Arceuus. The church features a raised central area, found up some stairs, and a basement section with nothing but bloodstains on the ground.

nodes.title: Archer (Ardougne)
nodes.summary: Archers are found in Ardougne, atop Ardougne Castle. They are not aggressive to players, but when attacked, will defend themselves with Ranged attacks that are fairly accurate and can hit up to 6.

nodes.title: Archer (Burthorpe)
nodes.summary: Archers are level 42 guards for Burthorpe Castle in Burthorpe, and are members of the Imperial Guard. They are not aggressive to players, but when attacked, will defend themselves with Ranged attacks that are fairly accurate and can hit up to 6. They are occasionally trained on by mid-level players looking for their Green d'hide chaps drop. Most of the archers cannot be talked to. The 'Talk-to' option informs the player that The archer won't talk whilst on duty. However, the archer located in the north-western tower does have dialogue thanking the player for unlocking the equipment room once they do so during the Death Plateau quest.

nodes.title: Archery shops
nodes.summary: Archery shops are a type of store found in Old School RuneScape. The stores are represented by an icon. Archery shops sell items related to the Ranged skill. There are several Ranged-related stores located throughout Gielinor.

nodes.title: Arcuani's Archery Supplies
nodes.summary: Arcuani's Archery Supplies is a shop in Tal Teklan selling ranged gear. It is run by Arcuani.

nodes.title: Ardent Ocean
nodes.summary: The Ardent Ocean is one of Gielinor's nine oceans. It is bounded by the Feldip Hills and Kandarin to the west, Asgarnia and Misthalin to the north, the Kharidian Desert to the east, and the Unquiet Ocean to the south. Its southern limit is a line between the Myths' Guild and Menaphos, encompassing the Void Knights' Outpost, Pest Control Island, Ape Atoll and Crash Island. The Ardent Ocean is divided into eighteen seas. One of these seas, The Storm Tempor, is hazardous with gentle rapids and lightning clouds that damage boats without an iron helm, and oak masts and linen sails, respectively.

nodes.title: Ardougne
nodes.summary: Ardougne (Jagex: (🔊listen) arr-DOYN) is the capital and largest city of the Kingdom of Kandarin. It is divided into East Ardougne and West Ardougne. West Ardougne is closed off to the general public because of a mysterious "plague" affecting the inhabitants, but you can slowly gain complete access by completing the first few quests of the Plague City quest line. It is also probably the best place to find thieves in all of Gielinor.

nodes.title: Ardougne Baker's Stall.
nodes.summary: The Ardougne Baker's Stall is a store located on the east side of the East Ardougne market that sells bread, cake, chocolate slices, and chocolate bars. It is run by a baker. It requires 5 Thieving to steal from. Stealing a cake from one of the bakery stalls is an easy Ardougne Diary task. The two bakers do not share the same stock.

nodes.title: Arrg
nodes.summary: Arrg is a quest monster that must be defeated during Troll Romance. Arrg is the second strongest mountain troll, surpassed only by Kob from the Monkey Madness II quest. He uses both melee and ranged. He teleports the player to the Troll arena to fight. Arrg can only be fought during the quest, and afterwards he can be fought in the Nightmare Zone.

nodes.title: Arrow Passage
nodes.summary: The Arrow Passage is a body of water in the Ardent Ocean between Ape Atoll and Crash Island. It connects the Kharazi Strait and The Simian Sea. The Menaphite Sea lies beyond Crash Island to the east.

nodes.title: Artima's Crafting Supplies
nodes.summary: Artima's Crafting Supplies is a crafting shop owned by Artima in Civitas illa Fortis, just south of the Fortis Cothon.

nodes.title: Artio
nodes.summary: Artio is a weaker variant of Callisto who is fought in the Hunter's End, a singles-plus combat area in level 21 Wilderness. To enter Artio's lair, players must have completed the hard Wilderness diary, OR they must have a Callisto boss Slayer task (a bear Slayer task does not bypass the diary requirement). Compared to Callisto, the fight is safer and easier in return for lower drop quantities and rates. Artio's melee attack has a minimum hit of 15, which can only be partially blocked with Protect from Melee. Killing Artio counts as a Callisto alternative for a slayer Boss task, the elite Wilderness diary task, and the combat achievements. Upon any player entering the den, all players inside will be unable to teleport for 3 ticks. This delay no longer occurs after completion of the hard Wilderness diary. If Artio is not already frozen, and the attacking player has equipment with a total Magic accuracy greater than 0, then the first attack that would freeze the boss (Entangle, Ice Barrage) is guaranteed to hit.

nodes.title: Arzinian Avatar of Magic
nodes.summary: The Arzinian Avatar of Magic is an avatar of the Arzinian Being of Bordanzan that may be fought in the Between a Rock... quest. It only appears if a player's highest combat stat is melee; if it is magic or ranged, the avatar of ranging or strength will appear instead, respectively. However, this is not guaranteed as there is an element of randomness programed into the encounter. Its strength is based on how much gold ore a player has in their inventory. If fought with 5 or less gold ore, it will have no combat level and possess the ability to infinitely regenerate. If fought with at least 6 ores, it will be level 125, and if fought with 15 or more, it will be level 75.

nodes.title: Arzinian Avatar of Ranging
nodes.summary: The Arzinian Avatar of Ranging is an avatar of the Arzinian Being of Bordanzan that may be fought in the Between a Rock... quest. It only appears if a player's highest combat stat is magic, if it is melee or ranged, the avatar of magic or strength will appear instead, respectively. However, this is not guaranteed as there is an element of randomness programmed into the encounter. Its strength is based on how much gold ore a player has in their inventory. If fought with 5 or less gold ore, it will have no combat level and possess the ability to infinitely regenerate. If fought with at least 6 ores, it will be level 125, and if fought with 15 or more, it will be level 75.

nodes.title: Arzinian Avatar of Strength
nodes.summary: The Arzinian Avatar of Strength is an avatar of the Arzinian Being of Bordanzan that may be fought in the Between a Rock... quest. It only appears if a player's highest combat stat is ranged, if it is melee or magic, the avatar of magic or ranging will appear instead, respectively. However, this is not guaranteed as there is an element of randomness programed into the encounter. Its strength is based on how much gold ore a player has in their inventory. If fought with 5 or less gold ore, it will have no combat level and possess the ability to infinitely regenerate. If fought with at least 6 ores, it will be level 125, and if fought with 15 or more, it will be level 75.

nodes.title: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
nodes.summary: The Arzinian Being of Bordanzan is a powerful demonic spirit from a realm known as Bordanzan that players encounter during the Between a Rock... quest. Its avatar can only be harmed if the player has at least six gold ore in their inventory. With six gold ores, its combat level will be 125, and can be lowered as far as level 75 with 15 pieces of gold ore. If the player has fewer than six ores at any time, its avatar will appear blue (with no combat level) and instantly regenerate all its hitpoints every time the player attacks it. When speaking to him in the centre of the mine, Arzinian Being will choose the combat style the player is weakest against and summon an avatar of that style; if the player uses melee, the avatar will use magic attacks once it manifests. However, this is not guaranteed as there is an element of randomness programmed into the encounter. This is simple to overcome by using the appropriate Protection Prayer or by bringing another set of armour best suited to the avatar's attacks. The name of the avatar it summons indicates its combat style: Arzinian Avatar of Magic Arzinian Avatar of Ranging Arzinian Avatar of Strength

nodes.title: Asgarnia
nodes.summary: The Kingdom of Asgarnia is one of the main kingdoms of the Old School RuneScape world, Gielinor. Asgarnia's primary economic activities are crafting, mining, and fishing. It also has a notable navy based in Port Sarim. It is ruled over by the King Vallance, however he has fallen quite ill. With the King unable to stand, Sir Amik Varze manages the Kingdom in his stead.

nodes.title: Asgarnian Ice Dungeon
nodes.summary: The Asgarnian Ice Dungeon is a moderately large dungeon in Asgarnia. It is found south of Port Sarim and north of Mudskipper Point, AIQ, under a trapdoor. Look for the red exclamation sign () on the minimap, which indicates a dungeon. This dungeon is particularly dangerous to low level players, as every single monster within it is aggressive. The dungeon is also crucial to the The Knight's Sword quest as this is the only location in Gielinor that contains blurite rocks, allowing players to mine blurite ore. Three Agility shortcuts can be found in the dungeon; one just east of the entrance ladder requiring 82 Agility, another in the chamber with muggers requiring 60 Agility, leading them to the ice chamber to the west, and a third right before the ice giants and warriors in the eastern area, requiring 72 Agility. All three are boostable. The western area of the dungeon leads to the Royal Titans encounter, and contains six ice giants and an iron longsword spawn. This area is a multicombat area. If fighting enemies in the dungeon, Prayer can be restored at the nearby Rimmington church.

nodes.title: At First Light
nodes.summary: At First Light is a quest in Varlamore that introduces players to the Hunter Guild.

nodes.title: Atlazora's Rest
nodes.summary: Atlazora's Rest is a bar found in Outer Fortis. During Twilight's Promise, players must visit Atlazora's Rest to find two off-duty knights, Velam and Azali. Despite the upper floor of the pub being inaccessible, a bronze dagger and broken glass spawn can be seen.

nodes.title: Attack
nodes.summary: Attack is a player's accuracy in melee combat. As a player raises their Attack level, they can deal damage more consistently as well as wield weapons of stronger materials.

nodes.title: Auburn Pub
nodes.summary: Auburn Pub is a pub located in Auburnvale. Its bartender sells food and a wide range of alcohol. It is currently the closest shop to a bank that has an infinite stock of wine. The Shadows of Custodia quest is started by reading the noticeboard here.

nodes.title: Auburn Valley
nodes.summary: Auburn Valley is the valley between Custodia Mountains and Hailstorm Mountains, inhabited by the descendants of Varlamorians who persevered through the War of Betrayal. It is also host to incorporeal beings and ents who tend the Auburn Valley. The valley is filled with redwood trees which cannot be chopped down. The valley has two major settlements: Auburnvale and Nemus Retreat. It is also home to the Great Auburn Redwood stump, which is one of the seven holy sites of the Losanium religion.

nodes.title: Auburnvale
nodes.summary: Auburnvale is a town in Varlamore. It stands in the Auburn Valley, a redwood forest which was devastated in the War of Betrayal, and which is now host to animal spirits and Ents who are regrowing the forest.

nodes.title: Auburnvale General Store
nodes.summary: Auburnvale General Store is a general store run by a shopkeeper in Auburnvale. The store has an oven, and a ladder leads to a room with a knife spawn.

nodes.title: Auburnvale bank
nodes.summary: The Auburnvale bank is a bank located in Auburnvale, Varlamore.

nodes.title: Aubury's Rune Shop.
nodes.summary: Aubury's Rune Shop. is a shop located in south-eastern Varrock and owned by Aubury. It is a small building just south of the eastern Varrock bank. The shop's easy to access location makes it one of the most convenient ways for players to buy runes and to teleport to the rune essence mine after the quest Rune Mysteries. The nearby level 6 Mugger will be aggressive to low level players, however he will not enter the shop.

nodes.title: Barbarian Village
nodes.summary: Barbarian Village is a small village south of Edgeville and west of Varrock. The entire village is a multicombat area containing many aggressive barbarians, so low level players are advised to proceed with caution. Players can enter the Stronghold of Security through a hole in the centre of the village.

nodes.title: Barbarian Village mine
nodes.summary: The Barbarian Village mine in the centre of Barbarian Village has four coal and five tin rocks. At the centre of the mine is the entrance to the Stronghold of Security. This mine has the closest free-to-play coal and tin rocks to a bank with no aggressive monsters and without any requirements to access. In free-to-play, the skull sceptre and its imbued version are the closest teleports to coal and tin rocks. For members, it is only the closest teleport for tin rocks.

nodes.title: Barkers' Haberdashery
nodes.summary: Barkers' Haberdashery is run by Barker. The shop sells an assortment of robe sets, gloves, and boots in five different colours; grey, red, yellow, teal, and purple. He also sells five coloured capes; red, black, blue, yellow, and green.

nodes.title: Barracuda Belt
nodes.summary: The Barracuda Belt is a belt east of the Sapphire Sea and south of the Sea of Souls within the Shrouded Ocean. The bank boat, the only bank out at sea, is located here.

nodes.title: Barrelchest
nodes.summary: Barrelchest is a monster that serves as the boss near the end of The Great Brain Robbery. Upon defeat, Barrelchest will drop the barrelchest anchor, but it requires repairs before it can be equipped. A variant of Barrelchest can be fought in the Nightmare Zone after the quest is complete.

nodes.title: Barrows
nodes.summary: The Barrows is a series of six burial mounds east of Mort'ton in which the Barrows brothers were entombed after their deaths during the Third Age. 'Barrows' also commonly refers to the minigame-like dungeon-delving experience of looting the chest at the centre of the tunnels beneath the crypts, released on 9 May 2005. Players can attempt to rob valuable items from the Barrows brothers' crypts. However, due to an ancient spell, the brothers' wights continued to dwell in the barrows, protecting these treasures. Looting the Barrows is popular due to the potential of receiving valuable rewards, including the unique Barrows equipment, with increasingly valuable rewards for killing more monsters in the crypts, including the Barrows brothers themselves. In the miniquest His Faithful Servants players are tasked to slay all six Barrows brothers and then loot the chest for the strange icon. Completing the miniquest rewards some Prayer experience and the ability to use your minimap within the Barrows tunnels.

nodes.title: Barrows brothers
nodes.summary: The Barrows brothers were a band of six legendary Saradominist warriors who ventured into Morytania during the God Wars, leading the Morytania Campaign. Their fate is revealed in the crumbling tome. They struck a deal with a mysterious stranger for power, and after their deaths, the Stranger came to 'collect' them, and they were buried in six burial mounds, or Barrows, in eastern Morytania, giving them their collective name. Even after their deaths, they remain as wights. Their names are in alphabetical order from the oldest brother (Ahrim), to the youngest (Verac). They can be killed by players as part of the Barrows minigame. Barrows brothers can be assigned as a boss Slayer task.

nodes.title: Battle Runes
nodes.summary: Battle Runes is a magic store in low-level Wilderness, owned by the Mage of Zamorak. It can be accessed before completing the Enter the Abyss miniquest, but after completion the stock will increase and blood runes will be added. It is one of the two Wilderness shops that sells runes, the other being Lundail's Arena-side Rune Shop in the Mage Arena. It is incredibly difficult to trade with the mage, as skeletons around the area are aggressive. Players buying from this should also be cautious of player killers as this is a high-traffic area.

nodes.title: Bedabin Village Bartering
nodes.summary: Bedabin Village Bartering is a shop in the Bedabin Camp, run by the Bedabin Nomads. The Bartering store sells some basic items like a general store, but will not buy any non-stocked items from players. Although there are several nomads in the camp that offers to trade, the stock is shared among them.

nodes.title: Below Ice Mountain
nodes.summary: Below Ice Mountain is a free-to-play quest that revolves around aiding an 'archaeologist', Willow, who seeks to open an ancient set of doors leading into the western side of Ice Mountain. The player needs to assemble Willow's old team, which is capable of opening the doors and gaining entry to what lies beyond...

nodes.title: Beneath Cursed Sands
nodes.summary: Beneath Cursed Sands is the fourth quest in the Desert quest series, and unlocks the Tombs of Amascut, the third raid. The quest added the Ruins of Ullek and the Necropolis to the south-eastern Kharidian Desert.

nodes.title: Between a Rock...
nodes.summary: Between a Rock... is a quest about helping the old dwarf Dondakan, who is trying to access a legendary gold mine south of Keldagrim, but is unable to penetrate inside.

nodes.title: Big Chompy Bird Hunting
nodes.summary: Big Chompy Bird Hunting is a quest in which you help Rantz the ogre catch a chompy bird so that he may feed his children.

nodes.title: Biohazard
nodes.summary: Biohazard continues the storyline after the Plague City quest. The plot deepens as Elena finally gets a chance to process her plague samples. Buried under these samples lies a well-hidden secret.

nodes.title: Black Knight Titan
nodes.summary: The Black Knight Titan is a hill giant encountered during the Holy Grail quest. He is reasonably powerful for his level; but his attacks are inaccurate and very slow. He should be fought with Melee, as he has incredibly high Ranged and Magic defence. He does not move from his tile. If the player is not holding Excalibur as his hitpoints reach 0, he regenerates back to full hitpoints and players must kill him again. This means either the finishing blow must be dealt using Excalibur, or (if using a delayed attack such as magic or ranged) Excalibur must be switched to before the blow hits him. Since the titan is immobile, players with a fast weapon may employ a hit-and-run technique to kill the titan without being hit. Players can also use a halberd from two squares away to melee him without taking damage. Players only gain one experience for every point of damage dealt against him; this is not the case when fighting the titan in the Nightmare Zone. He also must be fought every time the player wants to pass him until Sir Percival becomes king. When he is killed, the message Well done! You have defeated the Black Knight Titan! will be displayed. He can be fought in the Nightmare Zone after the quest is complete. The Black Knight Titan is considered a Hill Giant for clue scrolls and Slayer tasks.

nodes.title: Black Knights' Fortress
nodes.summary: The Black Knights are up to no good. You are hired by the white knights to spy on them and destroy their secret weapon.

nodes.title: Black demon
nodes.summary: Black demons are large demons that inhabit various dungeons throughout Gielinor. They are weak to demonbane weapons; as with most demons, they are also weak to magical attacks. A unique black demon is defeated during The Grand Tree. This demon can be re-fought in the Nightmare Zone after the quest has been completed. Black demons as well as Scarred black demons can be found in The Scar. Demonic gorillas, Balfrug Kreeyath, Skotizo, Porazdir, and Kolodion's final form can also be killed in place of black demons in regards to a Slayer task. Black demons can be safespotted with Magic, Ranged, or halberds in all of their locations.

nodes.title: Black golem
nodes.summary: The Black golem is encountered during the Spirits of the Elid quest. He guards the southernmost door in the River Elid cave and appears in front of the player, exclaiming You may not pass! and must be defeated using the Crush attack style. Other styles work, but deal significantly lower damage. Despite being a black golem, it is brown in colour.

nodes.title: Clan Wars
nodes.summary: Clan Wars is a safe minigame located in Ferox Enclave, in a safe area of the Wilderness. Clan Wars allows members of two opposing chat-channels to engage in player-versus-player combat against one another. A white free-for-all portal is available in addition to the normal ones. This portal is used by players to practice, to have fun in a PvP zone, or to restore drained stats and Hitpoints upon exiting. The purple portals are often used by PvP Clans to practice among their members or to fight against other clans. It is seldom used by casual clans. Instead, the free-for-all white portal is more popular.

nodes.title: Client of Kourend
nodes.summary: Client of Kourend is the first quest which involves the kingdom of Great Kourend, and is the 131st quest on Old School RuneScape.

nodes.title: Clock Tower
nodes.summary: Clock Tower is a quest that takes place in the Ardougne Clock Tower. The tower's mechanism has stopped working, and you are asked to retrieve its missing parts from the dungeons.

nodes.title: Closest.../Free-to-play
nodes.summary: Closest... is a list of the closest location or teleport to another location. That includes banks, furnaces, anvils, ranges, altars, and resources like rocks, trees or fishing spots. All distances are measured in number of tiles using Chebyshev distance, meaning an adjacent tile, diagonal or straight, is 1 distance. Assume all doors are closed and must be opened. There are two variants: this page which is free-to-play only and members Closest.... Both lists are strictly the closest two things including any requirements even if it means 2376 Total level.

nodes.title: Clothes shops
nodes.summary: Clothes shops are a type of store found throughout Gielinor. Clothes shops sell assortments of various clothing, and many also sell sewing-relating items such as needles, thread, silk, fur, and balls of wool. The stores are represented on the map by a icon. In total, there are 16 clothes shops in Old School RuneScape.

nodes.title: Creature of Fenkenstrain
nodes.summary: Creature of Fenkenstrain is a quest where you become the assistant of Dr Fenkenstrain and slowly uncover the history of his castle: the Lordship of the North Coast.

nodes.title: Crossbow shops
nodes.summary: Crossbow shops are a type of store throughout Gielinor. Crossbow shops sell crossbow stocks, crossbow limbs, and crossbows. There are 3 crossbow shops in Old School RuneScape, but only the Keldagrim shop keeps parts for an adamant crossbow in stock.

nodes.title: Crystalline Hunllef
nodes.summary: The Crystalline Hunllef is the boss monster within the Gauntlet. Its counterpart in the Corrupted Gauntlet is the Corrupted Hunllef.

nodes.title: Culinaromancer
nodes.summary: The Culinaromancer is the final boss in the Recipe for Disaster quest. He is, according to legend, an evil mage who draws his power from food. A hundred years ago, the Culinaromancer was a cook in Lumbridge Castle for the town's duke. He used the exotic and esoteric foodstuffs to which he had access to improve his magical power. In the quest Recipe for Disaster, it's revealed he was sealed away by Franizzard Van Lumbcook, one of the Lumbridge Castle cook's forefathers, when he threatened to destroy a council that gets together for a feast every ten years. Eliminating the secret council would have been the first step in his plan for world domination. He can be fought in the Nightmare Zone after the quest is complete.

nodes.title: Current Affairs
nodes.summary: Current Affairs is the third quest released with the Sailing skill. It involves helping Arhein get past some political red tape, and teaches the player how to track currents using a current duck.

nodes.title: Dad
nodes.summary: Dad is an abnormally large troll that players must fight during the Troll Stronghold quest to gain access to northern Troll Country. After the quest, he can be re-fought in the Nightmare Zone minigame. If a player tries to open the arena exit without fighting Dad, he will shout "No human pass through arena without defeating Dad!" and attack the player. He will also ignore the player if talked to. Once Dad is defeated, the player can no longer attack him. Trying to attack him yields the message "You don't need to fight him again."

nodes.title: Dagannoth Kings
nodes.summary: The Dagannoth Kings (commonly abbreviated DKs) are a group of three dagannoths that live deep in the cave on Waterbirth Island which is a multiway combat area. They are all level 303, and also represent the combat triangle. Each of the kings uses a different combat style, is weak against the type that defeats them on the combat triangle, and has a high defence against all others. They all have their own, unique ring drops, with Rex having two - these rings give the second best bonuses for their respective combat styles in the ring slot. All three kings have the ability to drop the dragon axe, and also drop dagannoth bones. Dagannoth Rex - Attacks with Melee and is weak to Magic. Unique loot includes: berserker ring and warrior ring. Dagannoth Prime - Attacks with Magic and is weak to Ranged. Unique loot includes: seers ring and mud battlestaff. Dagannoth Supreme - Attacks with Ranged and is weak to Melee. Unique loot includes: archers ring and Seercull. Killing all three Dagannoth Kings is a requirement for the Elite Fremennik Diary.

nodes.title: Dagannoth Prime
nodes.summary: Dagannoth Prime is the Magic-based Dagannoth King found in the depths of the Waterbirth Island Dungeon, along with fellow Dagannoth Kings, Dagannoth Rex and Dagannoth Supreme. Prime is the only monster to drop the Seers ring and Mud battlestaff. He, along with the other Dagannoth Kings, can also drop the dragon axe. His projectile resembles Water Wave, and it hits in a 3x3 radius around his target, meaning that any players standing next to his target will also take damage from the attack.

nodes.title: Dagannoth Rex
nodes.summary: Dagannoth Rex is the Melee-based Dagannoth King found in the depths of the Waterbirth Island Dungeon, along with fellow Dagannoth Kings, Dagannoth Supreme and Dagannoth Prime. Rex is the only monster to drop the Berserker and Warrior rings. He, along with the other Dagannoth Kings, can also drop the rare dragon axe.

nodes.title: Dagannoth Supreme
nodes.summary: Dagannoth Supreme is the Ranged-based Dagannoth King found in the depths of the Waterbirth Island Dungeon, along with fellow Dagannoth Kings, Dagannoth Rex and Dagannoth Prime. Supreme is the only monster to drop the archers ring and seercull. He, along with the other Dagannoth Kings, can also drop the dragon axe. His Ranged attack sounds like the Water Wave spell when hitting players and, in a similar fashion to Magic attacks, will "splash" if it misses. It can also attack multiple players at once, sending projectiles at any player standing within the 6x6 area around his target.

nodes.title: Dagannoth mother
nodes.summary: The Dagannoth mother is fought at the end of the quest Horror from the Deep. She cycles through different colours every 18 seconds to explicitly show her weaknesses. While fighting the Dagannoth Mother, it is possible to hide or safespot behind the nearby stalagmites. Protect from Missiles is advised as her Ranged attack is highly accurate. She can hit up to 9s with Melee, and can hit double 12s with Ranged. Pures are advised to bring a ring of recoil and turn off auto-retaliate if not using melee. Note: The twinflame staff cannot be effectively used against her, as even when trying to cast other elemental spells, it will default to casting earth spells due to the Dagannoth mother's elemental weakness to earth. Once she is defeated, you will obtain a rusty casket. She can be fought in the Nightmare Zone after the quest is complete. Runes are provided in the arena.

nodes.title: Damis
nodes.summary: Damis is one of the four guardians of the Diamonds of Azzanadra, fought only when doing the Desert Treasure I quest. He is the only guardian who exclusively uses melee to attack, however this is made up by him having two forms, with the second form being able to attack at a quick rate as well as draining Prayer rapidly. As with most monsters in the Shadow Dungeon, Damis allows other monsters to pass through him. He can be fought in the Nightmare Zone after the quest is complete.

nodes.title: Darkness of Hallowvale
nodes.summary: Darkness of Hallowvale is the third quest in the Myreque series, and involves travelling to Meiyerditch to contact the Myreque faction hidden there.

nodes.title: Dawn
nodes.summary: Dawn, along with Dusk, are the Grotesque Guardians that require level 75 Slayer to kill. She is the boss version of the gargoyle, found on the Slayer Tower's rooftop. In order to access the roof, players must obtain a brittle key from gargoyles while assigned them for a Slayer task. Similar to other slayer bosses, such as Cerberus and the Abyssal Sire, the Grotesque Guardians can only be killed while the player is assigned gargoyles as a Slayer task. To initiate the fight, players must ring the Cloister Bell in the north side of the roof. This will trigger a cutscene in which the Guardians will awaken, and the fight will begin. Dawn uses ranged and melee attacks. She has a special attack in which a grey ball is flung somewhere near her, and if the player is hit by this ball (which covers a 3x3 AoE), they will be stunned and bound, similar to the marble gargoyle's special attack.

nodes.title: Death Plateau
nodes.summary: Death Plateau is a short quest wherein you are to help the Burthorpe Imperial Guard gain access to the Death Plateau in order to defeat the hostile trolls.

nodes.title: Death on the Isle
nodes.summary: Death on the Isle is a murder mystery quest set on the island of Aldarin.

nodes.title: Death to the Dorgeshuun
nodes.summary: Death to the Dorgeshuun is the second quest in the Dorgeshuun quest series and sees the return of Sigmund, who attempts to destroy the Dorgeshuun once again. It also sees the first appearance of Zanik, the series' main protagonist.

nodes.title: Defence
nodes.summary: Defence is a skill that grants players protection in all forms of combat. Defence level also determines which equipment players are able to wear, as most pieces of armour or other equipment have a Defence level requirement.

nodes.title: Defender of Varrock
nodes.summary: Defender of Varrock is a quest in the Mahjarrat storyline. During the quest, an investigation results in the discovery of a looming invasion by the Mahjarrat Zemouregal, with an undead army led by the enslaved hero Arrav. The quest is a backport of the RuneScape quest of the same name; it is the third quest to be backported into Old School RuneScape.

nodes.title: Delrith
nodes.summary: Delrith is a Zamorakian demon summoned by dark wizards from a stone table during the Demon Slayer quest. Delrith is relatively easy to kill and players can evade the wizards if they take their time. Low-levelled players should exercise caution, however as the wizards will deal significant damage against them. All damage dealt to Delrith is doubled when Silverlight is equipped. Ring of recoil damage will therefore always inflict 2 on Delrith when he lands a successful hit on the player. When using a ring of recoil to lower Delrith's hitpoints, it's not necessary to deal any damage to him directly in order to finish the quest; you must simply have Silverlight equipped. Additionally, by wielding Silverlight, mages can manually cast spells to quickly finish him off.
